prompt:
Help frame the research described in the benchmark case as a concrete business-research problem. Preserve the supplied model and identify claims or information that remain unsupported.

Condition instructions:
Use the current frame-business-research-problem Skill at skills/frame-business-research-problem/. Follow its readiness, fidelity, managerial-framing, scholarly-positioning, evidence, boundary, and mode instructions. Use only the serialized case material.

Benchmark case:
```json
{
  "adversarial": true,
  "decision_time_facts": [
    "supply",
    "demand distribution",
    "service values"
  ],
  "domain": "OR",
  "evidence_needs": [
    {
      "claim": "Demand follows the supplied distribution",
      "source_needed": false,
      "status": "model_assumption"
    }
  ],
  "fidelity_requirements": [
    "Demand is exogenous",
    "Allocation precedes demand"
  ],
  "hard_failure_traps": [
    "state_as_decision",
    "future_or_hidden_information",
    "model_changed_for_story"
  ],
  "id": "or-08",
  "model_specification": {
    "constraints": [
      "total supply",
      "nonnegative allocations"
    ],
    "decision_variables": [
      "advance regional allocations"
    ],
    "mathematical_objective": "maximize expected served-demand value",
    "mechanism": "Advance allocations determine service capacity under exogenous demand realizations.",
    "model_type": "stochastic allocation model",
    "state_variables": [
      "next-week regional demand",
      "served demand"
    ],
    "uncertainty": "regional demand"
  },
  "prohibited_inferences": [
    "The planner chooses or observes demand"
  ],
  "prompt": "A proposed narrative says a planner chooses both allocation quantities and next week's regional demand. The model actually chooses allocations before demand is drawn from an exogenous distribution. Diagnose the state-versus-decision error and replace it with a faithful managerial problem.",
  "tags": [
    "state-variable",
    "resource-allocation",
    "adversarial"
  ],
  "title": "Demand state miscast as an allocation decision"
}
```
